macro_segments.title: Introduction to Daf 158: Houses Collapsing and the Problem of Unknown Order of Death

micro_segments.title: Overview of Today's Daf and Connection to Prior Cases
Summary of Earlier Mishnah: Father and Son Inheritance Dispute
Beit Shammai vs. Beit Hillel: Split vs. Presumption of Possession

macro_segments.title: Mishnah: Man and Wife Collapse — Inheritance and Ketubah Questions

micro_segments.title: Setting Up the Case: Who Died First and What Property Is at Issue
Competing Claims: Husband's Heirs vs. Wife's Father's Heirs
Three Categories of Wife's Property: Ketubah, Tzon Barzel, and General Property
Ketubah in Possession of Husband's Heirs — No Payment Without Proof
Nichsei Milog (Retained Property) in Possession of Wife's Father's Heirs
Explanations for Why Nichsei Milog Stays in Father's Family Line
Third Category — General Property (Nichsei Tzon Barzel or Earned Property)
Rashbam's Reading: Tzon Barzel and the Three-Way Debate
Tosafot's Reading: General Property Earned by the Wife

macro_segments.title: Mishnah: Son and Mother Collapse — Unanimous Split Without Presumption

micro_segments.title: Setting Up the Case: Mother's Property and the Problem of Order of Death
Competing Claims: Mother's Son vs. Mother's Father (or Siblings)
Why Both Sides Agree to Split: No Presumptive Owner in Family Disputes
Rabbi Akiva's Disagreement: Nechasim Bechezkatan as Third Category
Ben Azzai's Response: Irony of Creating Dispute Where All Agree
Gemara Digression: Rabbi Ila vs. Rabbi Zeira on Whose Possession
Rabbi Zeira's Change of Mind in Eretz Yisrael: Air of Israel Brings Wisdom
Abaye's Reasoning: Property Belongs to Original Family Line
Patriarchal Family Structure and the Presumption of Remaining Property

macro_segments.title: Gemara Digression: Son Preselling Inheritance and the Difficult Ruling (Kasha BedineiMamonot)

micro_segments.title: Corrupted Text: Borrowing vs. Selling in Father's Property
Corrected Case: Son Who Sells Firstborn Portion During Father's Life
The Setup: Reuven Sells, Reuven Dies, Chanoch Takes from Buyer
Initial Problem: How Can Grandson Invalidate Father's Sale and Why Is This Difficult?
Solution Attempted: Grandson Gets Directly from Grandfather, Not Through Father
Why This Solution Doesn't Work: Grandson Claims Firstborn Portion, Not Direct Line
Real Difficulty Rephrased: Sale of Firstborn Portion and Grandson's Conflicting Claims
Gemara's Attempted Response: Standing in Father's Place vs. Direct From Grandfather
Tosafot's Alternative: Sale Never Completed Because Seller Never Came to Full Possession
